assertions.title: Shared HTTP client is used across requests

steps.action: Read main.py lines 278-281 to verify app.state.http_client creation
steps.expected: httpx.AsyncClient instance created and stored in app.state.http_client

steps.action: Search for usage of app.state.http_client in routes
steps.expected: Routes extract shared_client from app.state and pass to providers
steps.observed: kiro/routes/openai.py:260 and :516 both extract shared_client = request.app.state.http_client

steps.expected: Client created once in lifespan, reused across requests
steps.observed: Client created in lifespan startup (line 278), stored in app.state for reuse. No per-request client creation found.

evidence.grepResults: Found app.state.http_client usage in main.py:278 (creation), main.py:425 (cleanup), kiro/routes/openai.py:260, :516 (usage)

assertions.title: HTTP client has proper connection limits

steps.action: Read main.py lines 265-269 to verify httpx.Limits configuration
Verify limits are passed to AsyncClient constructor

steps.observed: Line 267 comment: 'Limits: max 100 total connections, max 20 keep-alive connections'

evidence.existingTests: tests/unit/test_shared_http_client.py contains test_http_client_connection_limits() which verifies all three limit values

assertions.title: HTTP client closed on application shutdown

steps.action: Read main.py lines 421-427 to verify cleanup in lifespan
steps.expected: await app.state.http_client.aclose() called in shutdown handler
steps.observed: Line 425: await app.state.http_client.aclose() with proper error handling
Lines 423-427: try/except block catches exceptions during aclose(), logs warning but allows graceful shutdown
Line 422: logger.info('Shutting down application...'), Line 426: logger.info('Shared HTTP client closed')

evidence.existingTests: tests/unit/test_shared_http_client.py contains test_http_client_closed_on_shutdown() which mocks aclose() and verifies it's called

frictions.description: Server not running on localhost:8000 - unable to perform runtime verification of HTTP client behavior
frictions.resolution: Used code inspection and existing unit tests as evidence. All assertions can be verified through static analysis.

frictions.description: Command execution failing in Windows environment - unable to run pytest or Python commands
frictions.resolution: Relied on code inspection and grep results. Existing test suite (tests/unit/test_shared_http_client.py) already covers all three assertions.

summary: Tested 3 assertions via code inspection: all 3 passed. m2-a1 (shared HTTP client usage) verified through main.py:278 client creation and routes/openai.py:260,516 usage. m2-a2 (connection limits) verified through main.py:265-269 httpx.Limits configuration with correct values (max_connections=100, max_keepalive_connections=20, keepalive_expiry=30.0). m2-a5 (shutdown cleanup) verified through main.py:425 aclose() call with proper error handling. Server was not running, preventing runtime testing, but code inspection and existing unit tests (tests/unit/test_shared_http_client.py) provide sufficient evidence for all assertions.
